preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap (or trill) to a voiceless retroflex fricative occurs through a process of merger and assimilation, typically when a rhotic combines with a following dental sibilant, common in North Germanic and Indo-Aryan languages.

examples.note: The alveolar rhotic merges with the following dental sibilant to create a single retroflex fricative.
Eastern Norwegian dialects exhibit widespread retroflexion of clusters involving rhotics.
The RUKI rule caused dental sibilants to become retroflex when preceded by rhotics.

sources: Eliasson, S. (1986). The Interplay of Phonology and Morphology in Scandinavian Languages.
Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
